name: Red Sea / Suez Canal
summary: Day 85 (Sunday). **NEGATIVE-SPACE HOLDS DAY 6 OF 7 since May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed incident — UKMTO 'Recent Incidents' page shows NO second incident through May 17 morning IST.** If no incident by ~May 18 (Day 7), May 11 reverts to 'single unclaimed event' rather than start of new pattern. **The Houthi non-action persists through the weekend during which Trump-Iran is publicly hardening — Houthi quiet during a US kinetic-preparation phase is structurally significant intelligence, not coincidence.** Container freight FBX index update not available through weekend (weekly cadence). France Charles de Gaulle CSG continues Red Sea presence per prior. **PRIOR (May 13):** Day 81 — non-event POSSIBLY BROKEN May 11. UKMTO reported multiple explosions near a vessel ~130 km SW of Hodeidah; vessel continued voyage, no injuries; Houthis did NOT claim (CONFIRMED ACTION + UNCONFIRMED attribution). First Red Sea incident with possible Houthi attribution since Iran war's 2026 phase began. UKMTO maintains 'moderate' (not critical) for Bab el-Mandeb pending attribution. **NEW TRIGGER WATCH: second Red Sea shipping incident within 7 days of May 11 would collapse the Houthi non-action read.** Until then: single incident, sample size 1. France's Charles de Gaulle CSG remains in Red Sea / Gulf of Aden following CMA CGM San Antonio strike — first European naval-presence shift of cycle holds. Container freight FBX: Asia-USWC ~$2,127/FEU; Med ~$3,850/FEU (routing-penalty intact). Soufan coordination-decay thesis under first material test in 80 days but not yet falsified.

active_triggers.condition: Houthis attack commercial shipping in the Red Sea

recent_signals.headline: DAY 81 — RED SEA NON-EVENT POSSIBLY BROKEN MAY 11. UKMTO advisory recorded multiple explosions near a vessel ~130 km SW of Hodeidah on Monday May 11; vessel continued voyage, no injuries (CONFIRMED ACTION). **Houthis did NOT claim — attribution UNCONFIRMED.** First Red Sea shipping incident with possible Houthi attribution since the cycle began. New trigger window opens (7 days from May 11) — a second incident would collapse the Houthi non-action read. UKMTO 'moderate' threat level holds pending attribution. France's Charles de Gaulle CSG geometry unchanged.

FRANCE CHARLES DE GAULLE CSG TRANSITED SUEZ TO RED SEA / GULF OF ADEN FOLLOWING CMA CGM SAN ANTONIO STRIKE — FIRST EUROPEAN NAVAL SHIFT OF CYCLE; Macron called for Hormuz reopening AND spoke with Iranian President Pezeshkian (CONFIRMED — French armed forces, Naval News, ABC/NBC, US News, Washington Times). Paris: 'defensive step in full respect of international law' to 'reassure regional partners and support maritime security.' Day 76-77 of Houthi quiet sustained. FBX Med ~$3,850/FEU (+15% week — routing-penalty intact).
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED — French CSG redeployment multi-source (French primary + Western corroboration); Houthi quiet = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (UKMTO, Lloyd's List)
